How to Use RTG SolWatch
What is SolWatch?

SolWatch automatically scrapes solicitations from BidNet Direct, Bonfire, ESBD, CivCast, NTTA, and other Texas procurement portals. Each solicitation is scored for relevance to RTG’s civil engineering disciplines using keyword matching. The dashboard refreshes whenever a scrape is run.

KPI Cards (top bar)

Every card is clickable: it resets the filters, shows just that card’s sols, and sorts them by score (highest first).
All Solicitations — everything loaded, including past-due sols.
Open Solicitations — all currently-open sols.
New Today — sols posted today.
New This Week — sols posted within the last 7 days.
High Relevance ≥80 — the strongest matches.
All cards except All Solicitations exclude sols closing within 14 days (turn on the Closing Soon toggle to see them).

Filter Bar

Discipline — Narrows to sols that score above the relevance threshold for a specific discipline (Roadway, Drainage, Survey, etc.). The Score column updates to show the discipline-specific score.
New This Week — When active, hides all sols not posted this week. The green date highlight on Sol Posted is always shown regardless of this toggle.
Scraped Today — Shows only sols first picked up by today’s scrape (the Sol Scraped column).
Search — Filters by title, organization, county, city, or sol number.
Score ≥ — Minimum overall relevance score threshold.
Closes — Range slider to filter by days until closing.
Source / Org / Location — Multi-select dropdowns with search to filter by platform, issuing organization, or county. Each list has Select all / De-select all shortcuts at the top; Select all only selects the options matching the current search text.

Filter Presets

Presets are checkboxes you can combine. Within one group, checked presets broaden each other (a sol matching any of them shows); across groups they narrow (the sol must match every checked group). Example: checking RFQ & RFP plus Greater Austin shows only RFQ/RFP sols in Austin-area counties.
Sol Type — solicitation type filters (RFQ & RFP, On-Call Engineer).
Geographic Area — county-based regions (I-35 Corridor, Greater Austin, etc.).
My Lists — your Favorites (see below).
My Filters — your saved custom filters; the ✕ button deletes one.
Presets stack on top of any manual filters you have set — unchecking a preset puts you back where you were.

Save Custom Filter

The Save Custom Filter button sits to the right of Filter Presets. It lights up as soon as you change any filter (dropdowns, quick filters, search, score, presets) and is greyed out when everything is at its default. Click it, name the filter set, and it appears under My Filters in the Filter Presets dropdown as a checkbox you can re-apply any time. Saved filters live in your browser, so they survive dashboard updates.

Favorites

Every row starts with a star (Favorite) — click to toggle, click again to clear. Filter to your favorites with the My Lists checkbox in Filter Presets, or click the star column header to sort favorites to the top. Favorites are saved in this browser and survive dashboard updates, but they are per-browser: they don’t follow you to another machine and aren’t visible to coworkers.

Table

Click any column header to sort ascending/descending. Click a row to open its detail panel on the right. All dates are shown as YYYY-MM-DD.
■ bright green = posted today    ■ light green = posted within 7 days
■ blue = currently selected row

Detail Panel

Shows the full solicitation details: dates, location, estimated value, contact, description, relevance match reasons, and a per-discipline score breakdown. Click ✕ or the dimmed area to close. The panel scrolls independently from the table.

Download CSV

Exports all open solicitations (not just the current filtered view) to a CSV file that opens in Excel. Includes all fields: sol number, title, org, scores, dates, contact, and description.
